title: Introduction and Scope Definition
objective: Introduce the review's purpose, define the research domain boundaries, and acknowledge the limited evidence base comprising two papers from distinct domains (safety science and transportation research).

title: Methodological Approaches in the Reviewed Literature
objective: Survey and categorize the methodological approaches employed across the reviewed papers, distinguishing between unknown methods and mixed-methods empirical designs.

title: Safety Science Research: Knowledge and Expertise Development
objective: Examine the claims and evidence related to safety knowledge, expertise development, and safety culture in organizational and industrial contexts.

title: Pedestrian Behavior and Crossing Compliance
objective: Synthesize findings on pedestrian crossing behaviors, compliance rates, and the influence of physical infrastructure and traffic control on pedestrian decision-making.

title: Cross-Domain Synthesis and Limitations
objective: Assess the feasibility of cross-domain comparison, discuss the heterogeneity between safety science and transportation research studies, and identify limitations in the current evidence base.

title: Conclusions and Future Research Directions
objective: Summarize key findings, acknowledge the preliminary nature of this review due to limited sample size, and propose directions for more comprehensive future systematic reviews.
